reviews.review_title: A good try, but far too sparse
reviews.review_description:
Virtuoso is a great little introductory app for those who are just getting started in the world of electronic music production. As someone who has some experience using DAWS, however, it's baffingly limited. There simply aren't enough sounds, effects or features in the library to justify purchasing this at any price. Seeing the download size (132 mb) made me extremely skeptical, and my doubts proved true. The "instruments" are simply 5 different modalities of delivering the same incredibly limited library of sounds. Also, there is absolutely no way that this should exist without a step sequencer and a way to change tempo. What a huge oversight and omission. 

Also, I'm guessing that at 85% off, this has now been abandoned by devs and is on a fire sale by Meta. It's essentially a tech demo, but could be great as a baseline introductory into the barebones fundamentals of producing and recording electronic music.

Such a great interface and such poor functionality. Few sounds, few settings. No way to expand the sound library. The developer himself does not offer additional sound banks, there is no possibility to add something like VST plugins on your own. Half of the tools look more like a toy for children. It is difficult to integrate into some full-fledged workflow. It is very sad, because I have to admit that what is available now works just fine and is a really high-quality and interesting product with great potential. But right now it is more of a game than a tool for creating music. I will hope for further development, I wish the developer success!

the good
great fun synth toys, I wonder if they will ever update it and add a sampler, it uses helm vst for its sound but the format filter in the menu makes me wonder if the got the rights to use the code or did a clean room rewrite? for a sale price ok, it has some chord scales. background with cute mushrooms people.

the bad 
limited, no sampler, small amount of sounds, I would expect the audio made to be royalty free for me to use, no comment anywhere on this though, hate that there is no way to record the microphone but there is one, this looks abandoned and feels like it

conclusion I don't think I want to open this again not even to look at the cute mushrooms in the background. very mid think I made better music on my phone...

Virtuoso is a very fun game that allows you to be creative and do things in your own way where you can create music. The downside is, not like Auxy or something, you can’t set scenes or at least it must be quite complicated to,
So you don’t have an actual structure of a song, you just hit play and all the instruments that you’ve recorded start. Also fairly hard to navigate.
Very relaxing very rewarding only thing wish there was a guitar that's about it other than that really good worth the price would definitely recommend also is this game still getting updates
I use this app as a reward for private music lessons and we really need an update with some new instruments and environments!! Bring on Virtuoso 2 please!

The user interface on this one is fantastic. Buttery smooth response will please any musician when testing the musicality of this app.

The interactions with each instrument are interesting, and unique. I really loved the idea of grabbing the drums from what felt like a menu… and putting them where I want. It was simple to record, and set up quantizing. They put a lot of thought into user interaction and it shows.

The instruments aren’t necessarily ones you’d recognize, because they were designed to be played in VR, and not with articulated hand and finger movements.Its very similar to Patchworld in a lot of ways, except for the missing Multiplayer Mode, and build your own devices feature. Anyone who loves Patchworld will appreciate this app as well. Totally worth the purchase this app is beautiful and well made.

Packs a surprising amount of depth into as very approachable/intuitive interface. If you are into music and/or sound engineering, there's enough customizability to nerd out on... If you just want to play around with fun unique instruments, and maybe a looper? Hop in and have fun!
Either way it's the kind of experience where your curiosity and creativity will show you the way! Instant classic in my library.

reviews.review_title: Seems like there should be more
reviews.review_description: Intriguing demo but I'm not gonna lie it was kinda dumb after going through all the features, i feel like its missing a lot more settings and for its price I'm left very unsatisfied.

I'm a guitar and piano player and I am loving playing around with this app so far. I have 3 suggestions for the dev if he wants to add these things; 1) make it so that you can control the offset of the next row of notes in certain instruments. Right now the offset is set to 4 I believe but it would be nice to set number higher and have less repetition or lower and have lots of repetition. 2) Would be great to be able to expand the instruments to circle around the player, then you could sit in a rotating office chair and have a huge amount of notes to play around with. Another option for a similar set up would be if you're able to open two versions of the same instrument at once, then you could stack them on top of each other or place them side by side for more options and have two different sets of effects. 3) lastly it would be great to be able to save effects settings once you get a tone dialed in. Maybe you can already and I just haven't figured it out but that would be awesome. Great game so far and am loving the looping!!
I only have one foible with it, the microphones tool doesn't work great, it seems to not pick up on vocals at all unless you're shouting

reviews.review_title: This app is life.   Thank you.
reviews.review_description: Amazing,  and thank you so much for creating this.   This is my first app on my VR that I have used.
I have had this game for a while but I have been playing it more recently and it is very fun

This app has potential.👈 It's a slight learning curve but not a biggie. If you really like making music, you will do what you have to do. I just wish when you to go to record using the microphone that you don't have to constantly hold the button down, especially when you are hitting the pads. you only have one hand because the other hand is holding the microphone holding the button down. Hopefully, they will add more pads. Beats.maybe a voice changer. Other than that, this app is really fun to play! I can't wait to see what the next update has in store for this app. I hope it's not abandoned. Keep up the good work, Devs.👈😎🫡

developer_response.body: Hi! Thanks for the nice review! You can actually disable holding the microphone from a checkbox on its control panel. For performance reasons you can however only record one instrument at a time, so it might not help you if you want to record voice and drums simultaneously. Hope this helps make recording your voice a bit simpler at least!

reviews.review_description: I use Virtuoso for my home studio production. Specifically the percussion pads. It’s great but I’m stuck with the standard sounds. They’re ok, but what would be great is a MIDI interface. Apparently it exists somewhere but no real information on how it works. It needs to be simplified. An actual cable connection would be worth developing. If it had that capability it would be the ultimate tool.

developer_response.body:
Hi and thanks for the review! All information on how to use Virtuoso as a MIDI interface can be found on this page: https://virtuoso-vr.com/remote-control

For MIDI control, check out the Virtuoso MIDI Companion application listed on the page. We agree that it could be made easier to set up, but on the plus side the API is completely open source allowing you to use Virtuoso to control MIDI or anything else you can dream up. Hope you find some luck using it!

reviews.review_description: I bought this  many months ago and immediately refunded it. As a music producer/engineer, I felt it was too flawed in many ways. Decided to try it for free during the August sale (15 min trial). Holy $#@! The devs have clearly put tons more work into this app, and polished it up nicely. A lot more features, and much better functionality. Bravo, Devs! Please keep working on this and set the bar even higher as the premier music creation tool in VR.

Hey there's, my musical instrument is my VR glasses with Virtuoso app. The best tool I can imagine. It contains everything you need to create beautiful tracks. Some people think that electronic music has no soul but Virtuoso manages to create a bridge. Each instrument is unique and with the controllers of my Quest 3 it is possible to transfer all hand movements into the digital world in real time. Virtuoso has opened up a world of music to me that I would never have entered otherwise. 🎧

I hope you have fun like me with this app.

Have a nice day 😵‍💫

I bought this when it first came out and out of disgust from some reviews. I thought I would put my thoughts on here.

Yes, it would help if you had musical knowledge.  I don't, but you don't need really toto get a tune if you desired.

To be honest, you could count on Captain Hook's hand the number of decent tunes I've done. But I have done some interesting ones.

I look at it as a  musical sandbox, just making noise for the fun of it.

Mostly, it's all about experimental with the instruments.  If you start looking into the effects on them, you quickly learn what you can do with them.  Mastering them not so quick. 

It is my most played app,  have put thousands of hours.

It has a midi output for those who want it. 

Not going to go into all the instruments.

If you would like a workout, drums are the way . Can't remember how many you can pull out, but around 30, you can resize to be as large or small as you want. Put around your playspace and go crazy. I need a Swival chair now rather than physical.

It also has a mic on it, so can record on it.

Anyway, I better get off before I change my mind and don't post it.

Oh yeah, the range of sounds you can make is incredibly high.

I think this is game is good but it’s missing a few things 1: normal instruments like piano guitar and flute 2: some times Whalen I turn it on the sounds sound weird 3: it has made up instruments that are only electric not acoustic so ya that sums it up I think it s good but could be better😉🙂
This is certainly not for musicians. Sorry… lame
I know nothing about music and I managed to make a pretty good track first time. I did watch a few YouTube videos to learn some stuff...but in general really easy and user friendly.  Love it!

developer_response.body: Hey RoobieTuesday! Thank you for taking the time to leave this amazing 5 star review!

reviews.review_title: This is an outstandingly amazing app!!!!
reviews.review_description:
I love this app because it’s not just normal instruments and it has many variation of many different types of instruments. I also love how ABSOLUTELY easy ur is to make music in it! I first off thought it was going to be so extremely hard but it was actually pretty easy and I love the cool creations and probably millions of different sounds that you can make this the different instruments!!! If you are looking to getting into music and don’t really know if you fully want to get into it yet this is a great option! And even if you aren’t a beginner and already know if you want to be fully into the music career this is still a great app!!! 
And I just can’t get over the fact on how EASY it really is!!! 10/10 definitely recommend this app!!!

developer_response.body: Hey PFClegion! Thank you for taking the time to leave such a fantastic review.

reviews.review_description: I have nothing good to say about this except they will not refund you. It’s not what u think I’d stay away

developer_response.body: Hey JayNixxo, Your detailed feedback would be gratefully received. The Meta refund policy is up to 2 hours play time which means you have gone over this unfortunately. We have community Discord www.discord.gg/virtuoso you can join to leave ideas for improvements

reviews.review_description: I've never been interested in music and now I can create a real electronic music. It has a nice tutorial, easy user interface and many instruments. What I really need now is the DLC with classical instruments and electric guitar.

developer_response.body: Hey ZamknijMordeKrzysiu! It makes us so happy to read your review! Virtuoso wants to offer a new way to create music, there is why the instruments are mostly newly designed for VR and we do not have traditional instruments such as piano or guitar. Therefore Virtuoso does not currently have a guitar, however you might be able to import custom made sounds (PC version) by visiting the Modding section in our Discord www.discord.gg/virtuoso . We also plan to add new sounds and new instruments in the future, so keep an eye for future announcements in our Social Media!

reviews.review_description: There's no keyboard and it almsot seems as if they are trying to keep things avant-garde with all these weird instruments they have. Add a bloody keyboard!

developer_response.body: Hi Kammyland, we 're sad to hear that you were not pleased with your experience and we believe you might have had the wrong expectations about it. Virtuoso wants to offer a new way to create music, which is why the instruments are mostly newly designed for VR and we do not have traditional instruments such as piano or guitar. You might be able to import custom made sounds (PC version) by visiting the Modding section in our Discord www.discord.gg/virtuoso. We also plan to add new sounds and new instruments in the future, so keep an eye for future announcements on our Social Media!

reviews.review_description: I don't have any music experience at all. I have no clue how to make music and make a beat, but even if I can't, this game is just so much fun. It's really cool to experiment with the different sounds and instruments, and having the ability to upload and listen to other people's work is just awesome. I will continue to mess around even if I can't make a comprehensive beat

developer_response.body: Thank you a bunch for the 5 star review! If you're not already a member of our Discord, feel free to join (https://discord.gg/virtuoso). Here you can share your amazing beats, chat with the devs and the community, watch tutorials & much more!

reviews.review_description: What else needs to be said?

developer_response.body: Hey Pod9422! We think so too!! thank you very much for this review and letting others know how awesome Virtuoso really is!

reviews.review_description: The controls are weird the sounds are very limited the tutorials made no sense.

developer_response.body: Hey AquatikTruth! Could you please send us us an email over at hello@really-interactive.se. We'd love to help to understand and experience Virtuoso to the fullest! We look forward to hearing from you :-)

reviews.review_description:
Well my weekend life anyways lol it’s such an awesome experience to be able to make music in vr instead of on a flat screen like pc. I dock a star because while there is a tutorial it’s very bare bones. As soon as you finish the tutorial you’ll immediately realize that it taught you like 10% and everything else you have to just figure out thru trial and error. For example pick an instrument let’s say, the board then click effects. One of the things you see is a cube with a dot in the middle saying echo, you then have to play a single note with one hand while moving the dot around with the other to figure out how it works. Now I’m a musician so I know what delays/echoes ect are but in button or turn knob forms like on stomp pedals or sliders like in pc software but I have never seen a 3D echo cube thing before so a tutorial would be nice. A lot of stuff is new and different so lack of lessons makes it a little frustrating. 
HOWEVER Virtuoso just launched and I’m sure there’s still so much more and many changes and features in the future! 
I’ve only 2 suggestions for Virtuoso: 
1. more in-depth tutorials on everything.(YouTube Chanel maybe?)
2. Add the letters of the notes to the instruments. It’s fine for when I jam my own stuff but if I want to do a play along it’s harder not knowing what note is what. Example: so me where middle c is for example or all letters so I don’t have to struggle listening by ear to make cover songs. 
This note labeling is important to me because I can plug my quest into my mess boogie roadster and jam live on the fly solo or with others but it’s currently impossible because all the instrument keys ect are blank.

developer_response.body: Hi and thank you so much for the feedback, glad to hear that you are having fun with Virtuoso and you find it awesome :) We are planning to create more tutorials in the near future, however you can already find some videos both in our YouTube channels and in our Discord (www.discord.gg/virtuoso) where we are also sharing both official and non-official tutorials from the community. We aim for Virtuoso to be intuitive, and as fun for non-musician as it is for professionals, and that is why certain features (like notes and effects) are not immediately prominent in the menus. We however have in our plans to implement easier notes reading in the future, and improve the UI menu for the instruments!  Thanks for your patience and hope to see you in Discord, where we could help you more in discovering Virtuoso's features and tools.

reviews.review_description: Me and my daughter really enjoyed this game, super fun and we spend a lot of hours playing it so far.

developer_response.body: Hey Elsa-2013! Its great to hear you and your daughter enjoyed Virtuoso so much! Thank you for taking the time to leave a review

reviews.review_description: I like it and I’m ow my nephew will like it since he loves to make music 😁

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to leave such an amazing review! Feel free to join our Discord over at https://discord.gg/virtuoso & share your creations with us!

reviews.review_description: Bought just because I wanted to see how it worked as a music writing tool as I’m a working musician. Found this to be a boring platform, no real musical value that I can see. If you just want to make noise and generic music then it’s the app for you if you are thinking it could be a serious alternate writing platform then DO NOT BUY. Still hoping someone comes out with something that is worth the time and money for songwriters. I will be figuring out how to return this it will be first app I have returned on VR!

developer_response.body:
Hi BAMS1234, we 're sad to hear that you were not pleased with your experience and we believe you might have had the wrong expectations about it. Virtuoso is not a VR music studio, and it is not a VR export or real-life instruments. We also strive to target both professionals who want to have fun experimenting with music in VR and non-musicians: it is for this reasons that some "technical" music things might be more hidden within the menu and instruments. Said so, it is possible with time to use Virtuoso in a more professional matter (you can already connect it to your DAW with our MIDI companion app) and it is in our plans to improve sounds samples and import of custom patch sounds. We hope you might re-evaluate looking at it from a different perspective!

If you would like any help feel free to join our Discord (https://discord.gg/virtuoso), we'd love to help you get the most out of Virtuoso!

reviews.review_title: just needs a few more instruments
reviews.review_description: don't get me wrong i absolutely LOVE Virtuoso. you can make anything sound nice in it, there's no such thing as a bad beat. this game would have so much  more potential if they added more instruments though (guitar, piano, woodwinds, brass, etc). i still gave a 5 star rating because of how much music you're able to make with just a handful of instruments. i am aware of the fact that the game is already about a year old, but if the devs managed to implement their own version of the instruments listed (especially piano) then i could see this game being one of the top 10 best games of the YEAR. i really do hope that they add more instruments in the future, but im absolutely loving it so far! Devs, if you're reading this, please keep up the AMAZING work! i hope to see more in the future :)

developer_response.body: Hey! Thanks a bunch for the kind words and the 5 star review! We do have plans to implement new instruments in the future, however we have no exact date to share regarding this yet :-)

reviews.review_description: There is a lot of potential with this app. The flexibility is amazing. Not only the depth of the instruments but the ability to change and position them makes more complex motions easier. Add in the ability to record multiple tracks and you have a worthy studio to try out your creativity.  BUT...it has a tool that keeps your instruments in rhythm by setting the tempo and not allowing the instruments to sound outside the "beat." This is a real game changer for the drum pads. Setting up a rhythm track is so easy. Or, at least, it SHOULD be.  I found it dropping hits despite playing on the beat. That makes putting in a good drumline impossible. So it gets a refund. Broken.

I was initially excited getting this but then became extremely disappointed.  

I’m a professional drummer hoping I could play the kit I setup but it is poorly designed.  Sounds are cool but not able to truly play without accidentally getting strokes when it’s not my intentions.  The “sticks” are not accurate plus ergonomic is inaccurate.  Maybe I was expecting too much.  

I tried it, dissatisfied, returned it!!!

developer_response.body:
Hi masterbruce1400, we are aware of this issue & do plan to attempt to reduce the number of false hits from the drum sticks. As for the ergonomics, you can change the length and tilt of the drum sticks and the size of the pads to your liking.

We're sad to hear that we did not get the chance to help you out before you returned it. If you do decide to re-purchase please reach out to us at hello@really-interactive.se, we'd love to help with any further queries you may have!

reviews.review_title: A Great and Fun Musical Experience
reviews.review_description:
We first bought our Oculus at Christmas, and I immediately set about looking for a music app. I play piano among other instruments and I always enjoy new musical experiences. When I heard about Virtuoso, I could not wait for it to come out.

Virtuoso absolutely blew me away. The variety of musical instruments and experiences was exactly what I hoped for. The empads were a bit tricky to set up at first, but then I took off after that. 

The harp feature is by far my favorite so far. Custom setting your scale combined with the arpeggiation allows for some intense riff lines and improvisation.

I look forward to seeing how this app grows going forward. A fun first day for sure.

developer_response.body: We're so happy to hear you're loving making music in VR! Feel free to join our Discord (https://discord.gg/virtuoso) and share your creations with us! We'd love to hear them :-)

reviews.review_description: I love it so much! Are there plans for new Soundpacks?

developer_response.body: Thanks for the 5 star review! We are planning to add additional instruments but we do not have a date to share for this yet :-)

reviews.review_title: Great music app that has potential!
reviews.review_description:
Definitely a sleeper hit if you are looking for a music making software in VR.
I do wish there were some more instrument options like a rock organ or a guitar-esque string instrument.  Other fine-tuning things like making sure the loops time correspond to each other would be much appreciated. For now though, this is going in the right direction.

This is my possible rating. The tutorial froze 10 min after when saving the song. Exit the tutorial, there's no sound.
Will restart the whole thing. 
I will update the review if itngets any better.

developer_response.body: Hi Daeron, we're sad to hear you're experiencing issues. We are looking into what could be happening. As a start, could you make sure you have a healthy amount of free space on your Quest? Also, if you could join our Discord (https://discord.gg/virtuoso) or email us at hello@really-interactive.se, it would help us provide you better assistance and get to the bottom of this :-). Look forward to hearing back from you!

reviews.review_description: For one a step sequencer. A piano roll would be nice too. I know you want it to be a vr experience with vr instruments, but I would like to create more than 3 note sequences. Custom time signatures and a chromatic scale would be nice. I know you want this to be for non musicians but it is very limiting to real musicians
